content.problemStatement:
Vivek likes playing with lasers, but his friend Divyansh says that reading biographies is much more interesting. In order to prove that lasers are fun too, Vivek proposed an interesting task involving them:

You are given NNN points in a plane (numbered 111 through NNN); for each valid iii, the iii-th point is Pi=(i,Ai)P_i = (i, A_i)Pi​=(i,Ai​). There are N−1N-1N−1 line segments between them (numbered 111 through N−1N-1N−1); for each valid iii, the iii-th line segment is formed by connecting the points PiP_iPi​ and Pi+1P_{i+1}Pi+1​.

You should answer QQQ queries. In each query, a laser beam is fired horizontally to the right, from a point (x1,y)(x_1, y)(x1​,y) to a point (x2,y)(x_2, y)(x2​,y) (where it stops and does not propagate further). Find the number of line segments it collides with on the way.

The beam is considered to collide with a line segment if it intersects or touches this segment, except when the left endpoint of this segment is (x2,y)(x_2, y)(x2​,y) or its right endpoint is (x1,y)(x_1, y)(x1​,y).
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and QQQ.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.
Each of the next QQQ lines contains three space-separated integers x1x_1x1​, x2x_2x2​ and yyy describing a query.
content.outputFormat: For each query, print a single line containing one integer ― the number of line segments the beam collides with.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1:

For the first query, the beam collides with the 222-nd line segment and the 333-rd line segment.
For the second query, the beam collides only with the 111-st line segment. We do not consider it to collide with the second line segment, since its left endpoint is (x2,y)(x_2,y)(x2​,y).
For the third query, the beam collides with the 111-st line segment and the 333-rd line segment.
